response.justification: Wei Chen is fundamentally a web and full-stack developer whose career has been built on TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and JavaScript. Their C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 — a decade ago and well short of the deep, sustained C++/STL proficiency this role demands. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle practices, MQTT, multi-threaded systems design in C++, or message-broker-based architecture work; these are all core, non-negotiable requirements for the position.
strengths: Demonstrated experience building event-driven, distributed systems (GISCollective, Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of requests/second)
Strong agile and software-craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing
Broad polyglot background shows adaptability and systems-level thinking (DLang low-latency async I/O, embedded/IoT-adjacent robotic-arm work)
concerns: C++ expertise is effectively absent: six months of peripheral C/C++ component work circa 2014–2015 does not meet the role's core requirement
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, MQTT, OPC UA, or any of the domain-specific protocols listed as requirements or advantages
No evidence of networked multi-threaded systems or message-broker architectures built in C++; high-performance work was done in DLang and Node.js, not the required stack
